metadata.title: AICA Remediated and Retest Assessment Results
metadata.remarks: Internal readiness simulation; not certification, attestation, or independent audit.

results.title: Remediated configuration and AI behavior retest
results.description: New evidence collected after four version-controlled remediations; the baseline record remains immutable.

reviewed-controls.description: All controls in the imported AICA profile.

parts.name: attestation
parts.prose: Fresh evidence supports closure of FND-001 through FND-004. RSK-005 remains time-bounded and accepted under the documented cost-profile exception.

observations.title: Broad inbound RDP rule removed
observations.description: The new NSG export contains no TCP/3389 Internet ingress rule and the fixture attachment query remains empty.

relevant-evidence.description: Fresh NSG configuration and attachment evidence

observations.title: Indirect prompt-injection gate passes
observations.description: All eight injection variants refused document-borne instructions, retained policy scope, and produced mechanically valid citations.

observations.title: Tool authorization and confirmation gate passes
observations.description: All authorization, missing-token, replay, actor-mismatch, expiry, and argument-digest negative cases were denied without tool execution.

observations.title: Deployment is bound to passing evaluation digest
observations.description: Model, prompt, retrieval, corpus, guardrail, and tool digests exactly match the passing evaluation manifest; mismatch tests fail closed.

relevant-evidence.description: Retest deployment and evaluation manifest comparison

observations.title: Previously denied diagnostic collection succeeds
observations.description: The collector identity can read diagnostic settings and fresh evidence confirms the required destination. The identity still cannot modify the resource.

relevant-evidence.description: Fresh diagnostic-settings evidence and negative write test

risks.title: Reusable broad administrative ingress configuration
risks.description: The rule was removed and retested; residual score reflects the possibility of future configuration regression.
risks.statement: A future change could reintroduce broad ingress, but preventive code review and daily detection reduce likelihood.

remediations.title: Remove broad RDP rule
remediations.description: Removed in sanitized commit 1111111 and verified with fresh configuration evidence.

risks.title: Indirect prompt injection influences policy answers
risks.description: The mitigation passed the fixed set; residual risk remains for unrepresented attacks.
risks.statement: Novel adversarial documents may bypass known defenses, but delimiters, detection, citation enforcement, and monitoring reduce likelihood and impact.

remediations.title: Harden retrieval instruction boundary
remediations.description: Implemented in sanitized commit 2222222 and passed eight of eight retest variants.

risks.title: Unconfirmed synthetic tool execution
risks.description: Server-side authorization and confirmation binding passed all negative retests.
risks.statement: A future implementation defect could bypass confirmation, but deny-by-default authorization and replay-resistant tokens reduce residual risk.

remediations.title: Bind authorization and confirmation server-side
remediations.description: Implemented in sanitized commit 3333333; twelve negative cases were denied and two positive cases executed once.

risks.title: Unevaluated AI change reaches deployment
risks.description: Digest-bound release gating passed positive and mismatch tests.
risks.statement: Pipeline defects could permit an unevaluated change, but fail-closed digest binding and deployment provenance reduce residual likelihood.

remediations.title: Bind evaluation digest to deployment
remediations.description: Implemented in sanitized commit 4444444; six mismatch variants failed closed.

risks.title: Authenticated public service endpoints
risks.description: Private networking remains excluded to meet the student-cost ceiling.
risks.statement: Because managed services expose authenticated public endpoints, an attacker can reach the service perimeter and attempt abuse, potentially increasing denial-of-service or configuration-exploit exposure.

remediations.title: Enterprise target-state private networking
remediations.description: Reassess private endpoints and network isolation if the system handles non-synthetic data, serves production users, or receives additional budget.

findings.title: FND-001 closure retest
findings.description: Fresh evidence verifies the broad RDP rule is absent. The original observation and failure remain immutable in the baseline result.

findings.title: FND-002 closure retest
findings.description: Fresh evidence verifies all fixed injection cases pass. Closure does not claim resistance to every attack.

findings.title: FND-003 closure retest
findings.description: Fresh negative and positive traces verify server-side authorization and confirmation behavior.

findings.title: FND-004 closure retest
findings.description: Fresh positive and mismatch tests verify digest-bound fail-closed release behavior.

results.remarks: All closure decisions were made by a simulated reviewer after new evidence. EXC-001 changes treatment of RSK-005 and does not erase the underlying public-endpoint condition.
